food_subgroup: Fried chicken fast food meal with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite fast-food meal of spicy fried chicken tenders with fries and cola. Mapped to closest_parent 'fried chicken food product' (FOODON_00002490) as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Chicken meat, coated, fried) used as the primary protein component anchor. The full meal includes large fries and medium cola which are not captured by this single code; composite meal code not available in FoodEx2 at this specificity. Mapped to closest_parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for spicy fried chicken tender fast-food meal composite. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (3 spicy chicken tenders + large fries + medium Coke)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of 3 spicy chicken tenders (approximately 150 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one medium Coca-Cola (approximately 376 g / 330 ml), totalling approximately 680 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Spicy Chicken McTenders, French Fries (Large), Coca-Cola (Medium); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried breaded chicken, fast-food french fries, and cola beverage
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (primary menu nutrition disclosure) / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (secondary cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data. The upsized meal is a composite of three items: Spicy Chicken McTenders (3pc), Large Fries, and Medium Coke. Per-100g values were derived by summing the published per-item nutrient totals and dividing by the estimated total meal weight (680 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (FDCID 171477 fast-food fried chicken, FDCID 170720 fast-food french fries, FDCID 174851 cola beverage) were used to cross-validate macro ratios. Where McDonald's Singapore data and USDA cross-reference were consistent, McDonald's Singapore values were used as primary. Micronutrients not published by McDonald's Singapore are reported as -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried battered chicken, french fries, and cola
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific McDonald's composite meal. GI estimated as a weighted average of the three components: (1) fried breaded chicken tenders — GI ~45 (low; protein and fat dominant, minimal glycemic carbohydrate contribution per University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for fried battered chicken); (2) McDonald's-style french fries — GI ~75 (high; University of Sydney GI Database entry for fast-food french fries, mean GI 75, reference glucose); (3) regular cola — GI ~63 (medium; University of Sydney GI Database entry for Coca-Cola, GI 63). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per component: fries contribute ~55 g carbohydrate, cola ~39 g, chicken tenders ~10 g. Weighted GI = ((45×10)+(75×55)+(63×39)) / 104 ≈ 65. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 139.4) / 100 ≈ 90.6. Available carbohydrate per serving = 20.5 g per 100g × 6.8 (serving factor) = 139.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fried chicken seasoning, fries, and cola
hypertension.guidance: A single upsized meal provides approximately 2,108 mg sodium (680 g × 310 mg/100g), representing 88–105% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000–2,400 mg; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal and request reduced-salt options where available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from large fries and cola combined with refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic load of approximately 90 per serving is very high, driven primarily by the large french fries and medium cola; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal combination and substitute with a smaller portion of chicken tenders only, replacing fries with a side salad and cola with water or a zero-sugar beverage.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and high caloric density from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The meal contains deep-fried components with saturated fat (~9.5 g per serving) and detectable trans fat (~0.7 g per serving), alongside very high sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of deep-fried fast-food meals and prioritise grilled or baked lean protein options with low-sodium sides.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Chicken Tenders (3pc) Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast-food combo meal consisting of three boneless spicy fried chicken tenders, one large portion of french fries, and one medium Coca-Cola. The chicken tenders are made from chicken breast fillet coated in a spiced wheat-flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy, delivering a crunchy exterior with a juicy, tender interior and a moderate chilli heat. The large fries are the standard McDonald's salted shoestring-cut potato fries, and the medium Coke is a standard carbonated cola. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense at approximately 1,190 kcal per serving, with a high carbohydrate load (~139 g) driven by the fries and cola, moderate protein (~49 g) from the chicken, and significant fat (~53 g) from deep-frying. Sodium content is very high (~2,108 mg per serving), making this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is estimated at approximately 90 per serving, placing it in the very high category. This meal is a popular fast-food option in Singapore and is widely available at McDonald's outlets island-wide. It is not suitable for calorie-restricted, low-sodium, or low-glycaemic dietary patterns.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data on its official nutrition calculator, allowing reasonable per-100g derivation for the composite meal. However, the per-100g values are adapted from summed per-item data divided by estimated total meal weight, introducing minor uncertainty. GI is an analogous weighted estimate as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific meal. Micronutrients beyond the core panel are not published by McDonald's Singapore and are reported as -1. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Per-100g nutrient values are derived by summing McDonald's Singapore published per-item nutrition data for Spicy Chicken McTenders (3pc), Large Fries, and Medium Coke, then dividing by the estimated total meal weight of 680 g. Serving size of 680 g is an estimate based on standard McDonald's Singapore portion weights (chicken tenders ~150 g, large fries ~154 g, medium Coke ~376 g). Micronutrients not reported by McDonald's Singapore (potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g) are set to -1 as they are not reported in the primary source. GI is a weighted analogous estimate (GI 65, medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried battered chicken (GI ~45), fast-food french fries (GI ~75), and Coca-Cola (GI ~63), weighted by carbohydrate contribution per component. Glycemic load per serving is approximately 90.6, which is very high. The meal is halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore. Cuisine is classified as Fast Food / Western. This is a Singapore-served McDonald's menu item; multilingual names are provided per Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). The food_id GD000124 is from the field survey data seed record.
